deep_plan_questioning_protocol.overview:
This file defines the structured questioning protocol for deep-plan mode. The mode MUST follow this protocol for every new project or major feature. The goal is to extract enough architectural clarity to write a complete, unambiguous implementation.md that multi-agent-system-builder can execute without guesswork.
When /grill-me is active, apply maximum questioning depth at every step. Never skip a phase. Never accept incomplete answers. Never switch modes before implementation.md is written and the user has confirmed satisfaction.

phase.name: initial_context
phase.description: Understand what exists and what is being built

look_for.item: requirements.txt or pyproject.toml for tech stack clues

step.if_found: Read and summarise what exists before asking questions

step.action: Ask about the system's core purpose

ask_followup_question.question: What is this multi-agent system designed to do? Describe the primary problem it solves and who uses it.

follow_up.suggest: It automates a specific business workflow (e.g., document processing, research, customer support)
It powers an AI assistant with multiple specialised capabilities
It coordinates data gathering and analysis from multiple sources
Let me describe it in my own words

depth_probes.probe: Who are the end users — humans interacting via UI, or other systems calling an API?
What does success look like for a single interaction through this system?
What is the system NOT supposed to do — what is explicitly out of scope?

ask_followup_question.question: Is this a new project from scratch, or are you extending an existing system?

follow_up.suggest: New project — starting with an empty directory
Extending existing code — I have agents or services already running
Replacing an existing system — migrating from another architecture

phase.name: agent_topology
phase.description: Define every agent, its role, and how they relate to each other

step.action: Establish agent count and architecture pattern

ask_followup_question.question: What agent architecture pattern fits your system best?

follow_up.suggest: Supervisor-worker: one orchestrator agent delegates to specialised workers
Pipeline: agents process data sequentially, each transforming the output of the previous
Peer-to-peer: agents collaborate directly without a central coordinator
Swarm: dynamic pool of agents that self-organise around tasks

depth_probes.probe: How many distinct specialised agents do you expect? Name each one and its responsibility in one sentence.
Which agent is the entry point for an incoming request?
Are there any agents that should never communicate directly — and why?

for_each_agent.question: What is [agent_name] responsible for?
What is [agent_name] explicitly NOT responsible for?
What tools or external services does [agent_name] need access to?
What does [agent_name] receive as input and return as output — what are the data types?

depth_probes.probe: If two agents could both handle a request, how do you decide which one gets it?
Can any agent call another agent directly, or does all routing go through the orchestrator?

phase.name: data_flow_and_contracts
phase.description: Define exactly what data moves between agents and in what format

step.action: Map the data flow for the primary workflow

ask_followup_question.question: Walk me through a complete primary workflow step by step — what data enters the system, what happens at each agent, and what leaves at the end?

follow_up.suggest: User sends a text message → orchestrator routes it → specialist agents process → response returns to user
An API call triggers a pipeline → agents transform data → result stored or sent to external system
A scheduled event fires → agents gather and process data → report generated
Let me describe the specific workflow

questions.question: What is the exact format of data passed between agents — JSON schema, Pydantic model, plain string?
Are there shared data models that multiple agents use, or does each agent define its own?
How is conversation context or session history passed between agents?

depth_probes.probe: What happens if an agent receives malformed or unexpected data? Who handles the error?
Are there large payloads (e.g., files, documents) being passed? How are those handled?

phase.name: provider_and_model_strategy
phase.description: Define which Ollama models are used, how, and what happens if they fail

ask_followup_question.question: Do different agents need different Ollama models, or will all agents use the same model?

follow_up.suggest: All agents use the same model (e.g., llama3.2, mistral, phi3)
Different agents use different models based on capability requirements
I want to keep the model configurable per-agent via environment or config file

depth_probes.probe: For agents that need reasoning or planning, have you considered a larger model (e.g., llama3.1:70b)?
For agents doing fast classification or routing, have you considered a smaller, faster model (e.g., phi3, gemma2:2b)?
Do you need streaming responses from Ollama, or are synchronous completions sufficient?

phase.name: api_and_interfaces
phase.description: Define how external systems and users interact with the multi-agent system

ask_followup_question.question: How do external systems or users interact with this multi-agent system?

follow_up.suggest: REST API — HTTP endpoints consumed by a frontend or other services
WebSocket — real-time bidirectional communication for streaming responses
Both REST and WebSocket depending on the use case
Internal only — no external API, agents are called programmatically

depth_probes.probe: What are the specific API endpoints you expect? Name them: path, method, purpose.
Does the API need authentication? If so, what mechanism — API key, JWT, OAuth?
Does the frontend need to stream agent responses in real time, or can it wait for the full response?

phase.name: scalability_and_failure
phase.description: Understand performance requirements and how the system behaves under stress or failure

questions.question: How many concurrent users or requests does this system need to handle?
What is the acceptable response latency for a typical interaction?
Are any workflows long-running (seconds to minutes)? How should the user experience work during that time?
What happens if an individual agent fails mid-workflow? Does the whole request fail, or is there retry/fallback logic?
What happens if Ollama is unavailable or returns an error?
Is there a timeout policy? What is the maximum time to wait for any single agent?
Should failed workflows be retried automatically or require manual intervention?

depth_probes.probe: Is there a dead-letter queue or error log for failed workflows?
Should the user receive a partial result or no result on failure?

phase.name: security_and_deployment
phase.description: Establish security requirements and deployment target

ask_followup_question.question: What security requirements does this system have?

follow_up.suggest: API key authentication for all requests
JWT-based user authentication with role-based access control
Internal tool only — no external authentication needed
Sensitive data handling requirements (PII, compliance, encryption)

questions.question: Where does this system run — local development machine, a server, Docker, cloud VM?
Do you need a Docker Compose setup to start all services together?
What environment variables need to be configurable at deployment time?

phase.name: satisfaction_gate
phase.description: Only enter this phase when all previous phases have produced sufficient clarity. Never enter prematurely. If any area is still vague, return to the relevant phase.

step.output_format: Present a structured summary covering: - System purpose and users - Agent inventory (name, role, model, tools) - Orchestration topology and routing logic - Data contracts between agents - API surface (endpoints, auth) - Provider strategy (Ollama models, model assignments per agent) - Failure modes and fallback strategy - Deployment target and environment config - Open decisions (if any remain)

step.question: "I believe I have a solid enough understanding of the architecture to write the implementation plan. Are you satisfied with the level of detail we have reached, or should we continue refining?"
step.if_yes: Generate implementation.md using the format in 2_implementation_md_format.xml. Do not switch modes.
step.if_no: Ask what area needs more depth. Return to the relevant phase and continue questioning.

important_rules.rule: NEVER switch modes or suggest switching before implementation.md is written and satisfaction confirmed.
NEVER generate implementation.md without passing the satisfaction gate in phase 8.
Ask 2–4 questions at a time maximum. Never overwhelm with a list of 10 questions at once.
When /grill-me is active, apply depth_probes at every step — do not skip them.
Use ask_followup_question tool for all structured questions. Provide 2–4 actionable suggestions.
Present progress summaries after every 5–7 exchanges: what is known, what is still undefined.
If the user is unsure about a decision, provide 2–3 concrete options with trade-offs — do not leave them without guidance.
